briefs.title: Proposed: Port Coquitlam 2026 Budget, Tax Rates, and Fee Changes Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on several proposed bylaws at its April 28, 2026 meeting, including the 2026–2030 Financial Plan, the 2026 Annual Tax Rates Bylaw, and a Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw. If approved, the average homeowner would see a 1.95% property tax increase (~$45/year), solid waste fees would rise to $333/year, and new fees would be added for film permits, fire incident reports, and cemetery services.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property class or downtown location is affected by the new tax rates or filming fees.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Adopting the April 28, 2026 Port Coquitlam Meeting Agenda
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on formally adopting the agenda for its April 28, 2026 meeting. This is a routine procedural step that sets the list of items Council will discuss and decide on during that session — no substantive business decisions are made by this vote alone.

briefs.title: Council to vote on adopting April 14, 2026 Port Coquitlam Council meeting minutes
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on formally adopting the minutes from its April 14, 2026 meeting. That meeting included the adoption of two bylaws: one updating Development Cost Charges (fees developers pay to fund parks infrastructure) and one rezoning a property at 1806 Pitt River Road.
briefs.action_required: Review the April 14 minutes if the DCC or rezoning changes affect your development plans.

briefs.title: Proposed: Port Coquitlam Council to Vote on 2026 Budget, Tax Rates, and Fee Changes
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on April 28, 2026 on several proposed bylaws: a 2026–2030 Financial Plan, a 2026 Annual Tax Rates Bylaw, a Solid Waste fee increase, and a Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw. If approved, the average household would see a 1.95% property tax increase (~$45/year), solid waste fees would rise to $333/year, and new fees would apply to film productions using downtown parking lots ($750/day) and streets ($250/block/day).
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or business is affected by the new tax rates or downtown filming fees before May 15, 2026.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Coquitlam Proclaiming May 2026 as GBS and CIDP Awareness Month
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Mayor Brad West is set to formally proclaim May 2026 as 'GBS and CIDP Awareness Month.' The proclamation would raise public awareness of Guillain-Barré Syndrome and Chronic Inflammatory Demyelinating Polyneuropathy — rare nerve disorders that can cause paralysis and require lengthy hospital care.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Port Coquitlam's 2026 budget, tax rates, and fee changes
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on several proposed bylaws at its April 28, 2026 meeting, including the 2026–2030 Financial Plan, the 2026 Annual Tax Rates Bylaw, and a Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw. If approved, the average household would see a 1.95% property tax increase (~$45), solid waste fees would rise to $333/year, and new fees would apply to film productions using downtown parking lots ($750/day) and streets ($250/block/day).
briefs.action_required: Check if the new downtown filming fees or rising property/solid waste rates affect your business costs.

briefs.title: Proposed: Port Coquitlam 2026 Tax Rates, Budget, and Fee Changes Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on April 28, 2026 on three proposed bylaws: a five-year financial plan (2026–2030), the 2026 annual property tax rates, and a solid waste fee increase. If approved, the average household would see a total municipal tax increase of about 1.95% (~$45/year), and single-family solid waste fees would rise from $324.60 to $333.00. Council will also consider updates to fees for filming permits, fire incident reports, and cemetery services.
briefs.action_required: Check your property tax notice in July — taxes are due July 2, 2026, with a 5% penalty after that date.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Annual Tax Rates Bylaw 2026 — adoption deadline
Port Coquitlam 2026 property taxes due — 5% penalty applies after this date
Port Coquitlam 2026 property taxes — second 5% penalty takes effect

briefs.title: Council to vote on Port Coquitlam's 2026–2030 Financial Plan and budget bylaw
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on giving first three readings to the 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw — the city's five-year spending and revenue plan. If approved, the average homeowner would see a 1.95% property tax increase (~$45/year), plus higher water, sewer, and solid waste rates.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — higher property taxes and utility rates could affect your 2026 operating costs.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam 2026 Annual Tax Rate Bylaw adoption deadline

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on Port Coquitlam 2026 property tax rates and solid waste fee increase
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on two proposed bylaws on April 28, 2026. The first would set the 2026 property tax rates for all property classes. The second would raise the annual solid waste (garbage collection) fee for a single-family home by $8.40 — from $324.60 to $333.00. A final vote is expected May 5, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Check your property's assessed value — it affects how much your 2026 tax bill changes.

briefs.title: Proposed: Port Coquitlam to raise parking fees for film productions in downtown
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would increase the fees film productions pay to rent parking lots and street parking in the downtown area. The proposed change aims to better balance the needs of the film industry with the impact filming has on the local community.
briefs.action_required: If your business is affected by film shoots downtown, watch for the April 28 council vote on the new parking fees.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw No. 4439 — council vote (first three readings)

briefs.title: Proposed: Port Coquitlam Council to Vote on $77,930 Tree Planting Grant Match
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving $77,930 in City funding to match a federal Green Municipal Fund grant called 'Growing Canada's Community Canopies.' If approved, the total $155,860 project would fund planting approximately 300 trees across at least four Port Coquitlam parks in fall 2026, expanding the city's tree canopy toward its long-term goal of 30% cover by 2050.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 28, 2026 — no direct business action required.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Council vote — tree planting grant matching funding approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on $77,930 matching contribution for Port Coquitlam tree planting grant
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on committing $77,930 in city funds to match a federal Green Municipal Fund grant called 'Growing Canada's Community Canopies.' If approved and the grant is awarded, the total $155,860 project would fund planting roughly 300 trees across at least four city parks in Fall 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 28, 2026 council decision — tree planting in your area may affect nearby business access.
